mnemonics: Zoo Keepers Compare Lions & Tigers (ZKCLT): Z-test, Known variance, Compare, Large samples, Two-sample.
keyPoints: The two-sample Z-test is used to compare the means of two independent, large samples (typically n > 30).
It relies on the Central Limit Theorem, which states that the sampling distribution of the mean is approximately normal for large n.
The test statistic is calculated by finding the difference between the two sample means and dividing by the standard error of the difference.
commonMistakes: Using a Z-test for small samples without knowing the population variance (a t-test is often more appropriate).
Assuming equal variances without checking; this can affect the calculation of the pooled standard error.
Confusing the standard error of the difference for two samples with the standard error for a single sample.
